# Pre-Edit Checklist

**READ THIS BEFORE MODIFYING ANY FILE.**

## Quick Rules (memorize these)

1. **No `var`** except for: `int`, `string`, `bool`, `double`, `float`, `decimal`, `char`, `byte`
2. **Use `new ()`** not `new TypeName()` when type is on left side
3. **Use `[...]`** not `new () { ... }` for collections
4. **SubView/SuperView** - never say "child", "parent", or "container"
5. **Unused lambda params** - use `_` discard: `(_, _) => { }`
6. **Local functions** - use camelCase: `void myLocalFunc ()`
7. **Backing fields** - place immediately before their property (ReSharper bug, must do manually)
8. **SPACE BEFORE PARENTHESES** - `Method ()` not `Method()`, `array [i]` not `array[i]` (see `formatting.md`)
9. **Braces on next line** - ALL opening braces on next line (Allman style)
10. **Blank lines** - before `return`/`break`/`continue`/`throw`, after control blocks

## Before Each File Edit

Ask yourself:
- [ ] Am I using explicit types (not var)?
- [ ] Am I using target-typed new ()?
- [ ] Am I using collection expressions []?
- [ ] Are my lambda parameters discards if unused?
- [ ] Am I using correct terminology (SubView, not child)?
- [ ] **Did I add space BEFORE parentheses and brackets?**
- [ ] **Are ALL braces on the next line?**
- [ ] **Did I add blank lines before returns and after control blocks?**

## If Unsure

Re-read the relevant rule file in `.claude/rules/`:
- `formatting.md` - **SPACING, BRACES, BLANK LINES** (most commonly violated!)
- `type-declarations.md` - var vs explicit types
- `target-typed-new.md` - new() syntax
- `terminology.md` - SubView/SuperView terms
- `event-patterns.md` - lambdas, closures, handlers
- `collection-expressions.md` - [...] syntax
- `cwp-pattern.md` - Cancellable Workflow Pattern
- `code-layout.md` - backing fields, member ordering

## Task-Specific Guides

Check `.claude/tasks/` for task-specific checklists:
- `build-app.md` - Building applications with Terminal.Gui

Check `.claude/cookbook/` for common UI patterns.

1	namespace Terminal.Gui.ViewBase;
2	
3	public partial class View
4	{
5	    #region Content Area
6	
7	    // nullable holder of developer specified Content Size. If null then the developer did not
8	    // explicitly set it and the content size will be calculated dynamically.
9	    private Size? _contentSize;
10	
11	    /// <summary>
12	    ///     Sets the size of the View's content.
13	    /// </summary>
14	    /// <remarks>
15	    ///     <para>
16	    ///         See the View Layout Deep Dive for more information:
17	    ///         <see href="https://gui-cs.github.io/Terminal.Gui/docs/layout.html"/>
18	    ///     </para>
19	    ///     <para>
20	    ///         Negative sizes are not supported.
21	    ///     </para>
22	    ///     <para>
23	    ///         If not explicitly set to a non-<see langword="null"/> value, and the View has Subviews,
24	    ///         <see cref="GetContentSize ()"/> will return
25	    ///         the size of the <see cref="Viewport"/>.
26	    ///     </para>
27	    ///     <para>
28	    ///         If set to a non-<see langword="null"/> value, <see cref="Viewport"/> describes the portion of the content
29	    ///         currently visible to the user. This enables
30	    ///         virtual scrolling and the behavior of <see cref="DimAutoStyle.Content"/> will be to use
31	    ///         <see cref="GetContentSize ()"/> to determine the size
32	    ///         of the view.
33	    ///     </para>
34	    ///     <para>
35	    ///         This method follows the Cancellable Work Pattern (CWP). The <see cref="ContentSizeChanging"/> event
36	    ///         is raised before the change, and <see cref="ContentSizeChanged"/> is raised after.
37	    ///     </para>
38	    /// </remarks>
39	    /// <seealso cref="ContentSizeChanging"/>
40	    /// <seealso cref="ContentSizeChanged"/>
41	    public void SetContentSize (Size? contentSize)
42	    {
43	        if (contentSize is { } && (contentSize.Value.Width < 0 || contentSize.Value.Height < 0))
44	        {
45	            throw new ArgumentException (@"ContentSize cannot be negative.", nameof (contentSize));
46	        }
47	
48	        CWPPropertyHelper.ChangeProperty (this,
49	                                          ref _contentSize,
50	                                          contentSize,
51	                                          OnContentSizeChanging,
52	                                          ContentSizeChanging,
53	                                          _ => SetNeedsLayout (),
54	                                          OnContentSizeChanged,
55	                                          ContentSizeChanged,
56	                                          out Size? _);
57	    }
58	
59	    /// <summary>
60	    ///     Gets the size of the View's content.
61	    /// </summary>
62	    /// <remarks>
63	    ///     a>
64	    ///     <para>
65	    ///         See the View Layout Deep Dive for more information:
66	    ///         <see href="https://gui-cs.github.io/Terminal.Gui/docs/layout.html"/>
67	    ///     </para>
68	    ///     <para>
69	    ///         If the content size was not explicitly set by <see cref="SetContentSize"/>, and the View has no visible
70	    ///         subviews, <see cref="GetContentSize ()"/> will return the
71	    ///         size of
72	    ///         <see cref="Viewport"/>.
73	    ///     </para>
74	    ///     <para>
75	    ///         If the content size was not explicitly set by <see cref="SetContentSize"/>, this function will return the
76	    ///         Viewport size.
77	    ///     </para>
78	    ///     <para>
79	    ///         If set <see cref="Viewport"/> describes the portion of the content currently visible to the user. This enables
80	    ///         virtual scrolling.
81	    ///     </para>

1	namespace Terminal.Gui.Views;
2	
3	/// <summary>Shows a checkbox that can be cycled between two or three states.</summary>
4	/// <remarks>
5	///     <para>
6	///         <see cref="RadioStyle"/> is used to display radio button style glyphs (●) instead of checkbox style glyphs (☑).
7	///     </para>
8	///     <para>Default mouse bindings:</para>
9	///     <list type="table">
10	///         <listheader>
11	///             <term>Mouse Event</term> <description>Action</description>
12	///         </listheader>
13	///         <item>
14	///             <term>Click</term> <description>Toggles the checked state (<see cref="Command.Activate"/>).</description>
15	///         </item>
16	///         <item>
17	///             <term>Double-Click</term> <description>Accepts the checkbox (<see cref="Command.Accept"/>).</description>
18	///         </item>
19	///     </list>
20	/// </remarks>
21	public class CheckBox : View, IValue<CheckState>
22	{
23	    /// <summary>
24	    ///     Gets or sets the default Highlight Style.
25	    /// </summary>
26	    [ConfigurationProperty (Scope = typeof (ThemeScope))]
27	    public static MouseState DefaultMouseHighlightStates { get; set; } = MouseState.PressedOutside | MouseState.Pressed | MouseState.In;
28	
29	    /// <summary>
30	    ///     Initializes a new instance of <see cref="CheckBox"/>.
31	    /// </summary>
32	    public CheckBox ()
33	    {
34	        Width = Dim.Auto (DimAutoStyle.Text);
35	        Height = Dim.Auto (DimAutoStyle.Text, 1);
36	
37	        CanFocus = true;
38	
39	        // Activate (Space key and single-click) - Raise Activate event and Advance
40	        // - DO NOT raise Accept
41	        // - DO SetFocus (if focus is not desired, set CanFocus to false)
42	
43	        // Accept (Enter key and double-click) - Raise Accept event
44	        // - DO NOT advance state
45	
46	        // Use LeftButtonClicked instead of LeftButtonReleased to prevent double activation on double-click.
47	        // LeftButtonClicked fires once per click; LeftButtonReleased fires on each release (twice for double-click).
48	        MouseBindings.Remove (MouseFlags.LeftButtonReleased);
49	        MouseBindings.Add (MouseFlags.LeftButtonClicked, Command.Activate);
50	        MouseBindings.Add (MouseFlags.LeftButtonDoubleClicked, Command.Accept);
51	
52	        TitleChanged += Checkbox_TitleChanged;
53	
54	        MouseHighlightStates = DefaultMouseHighlightStates;
55	    }
56	
57	    /// <inheritdoc/>
58	    protected override void OnActivated (ICommandContext? commandContext)
59	    {
60	        base.OnActivated (commandContext);
61	        AdvanceCheckState ();
62	    }
63	
64	    private void Checkbox_TitleChanged (object? sender, EventArgs<string> e)
65	    {
66	        base.Text = e.Value;
67	        TextFormatter.HotKeySpecifier = HotKeySpecifier;
68	    }
69	
70	    /// <inheritdoc/>
71	    public override string Text { get => Title; set => base.Text = Title = value; }
72	
73	    /// <inheritdoc/>
74	    public override Rune HotKeySpecifier { get => base.HotKeySpecifier; set => TextFormatter.HotKeySpecifier = base.HotKeySpecifier = value; }
75	
76	    /// <summary>
77	    ///     If <see langword="true"/> allows <see cref="Value"/> to be <see cref="CheckState.None"/>. The default is
78	    ///     <see langword="false"/>.
79	    /// </summary>
80	    public bool AllowCheckStateNone
81	    {
82	        get;
83	        set
84	        {
85	            if (field == value)
86	            {
87	                return;
88	            }
89	
90	            field = value;
91	
92	            if (Value == CheckState.None)
93	            {
94	                Value = CheckState.UnChecked;
95	            }
96	        }
97	    }
98	
99	    #region IValue<CheckState> Implementation
100	
101	    private CheckState _value = CheckState.UnChecked;
102	
103	    /// <summary>
104	    ///     Gets or sets the state of the <see cref="CheckBox"/>.
105	    /// </summary>
106	    /// <remarks>
107	    ///     <para>
108	    ///         If <see cref="AllowCheckStateNone"/> is <see langword="true"/> and <see cref="CheckState.None"/>, the
109	    ///         <see cref="CheckBox"/>
110	    ///         will display the <c>Glyphs.CheckStateNone</c> character (☒).
111	    ///     </para>
112	    ///     <para>
113	    ///         If <see cref="CheckState.UnChecked"/>, the <see cref="CheckBox"/>
114	    ///         will display the <c>Glyphs.CheckStateUnChecked</c> character (☐).
115	    ///     </para>
116	    ///     <para>
117	    ///         If <see cref="CheckState.Checked"/>, the <see cref="CheckBox"/>
118	    ///         will display the <c>Glyphs.CheckStateChecked</c> character (☑).
119	    ///     </para>
120	    /// </remarks>
121	    public CheckState Value { get => _value; set => ChangeValue (value); }
122	
123	    /// <inheritdoc/>
124	    public event EventHandler<ValueChangingEventArgs<CheckState>>? ValueChanging;
125	
126	    /// <inheritdoc/>
127	    public event EventHandler<ValueChangedEventArgs<CheckState>>? ValueChanged;
128	
129	    /// <summary>
130	    ///     Called when the <see cref="CheckBox"/> <see cref="Value"/> is changing.
131	    /// </summary>
132	    /// <remarks>
133	    ///     <para>
134	    ///         The value change can be cancelled by returning <see langword="true"/> or setting
135	    ///         <see cref="ValueChangingEventArgs{T}.Handled"/> to <see langword="true"/>.
136	    ///     </para>
137	    /// </remarks>
138	    /// <param name="args">The event arguments containing old and new values.</param>
139	    /// <returns><see langword="true"/> to cancel the change; otherwise <see langword="false"/>.</returns>
140	    protected virtual bool OnValueChanging (ValueChangingEventArgs<CheckState> args) => false;
141	
142	    /// <summary>
143	    ///     Called when the <see cref="CheckBox"/> <see cref="Value"/> has changed.
144	    /// </summary>
145	    /// <param name="args">The event arguments containing old and new values.</param>
146	    protected virtual void OnValueChanged (ValueChangedEventArgs<CheckState> args) { }

487	    ///     main loop.
488	    /// </summary>
489	    /// <param name="views">The views to layout.</param>
490	    /// <param name="contentSize">The size to bound the views by.</param>
491	    /// <returns><see langword="true"/>If any of the views needed to be laid out.</returns>
492	    internal static bool Layout (IEnumerable<View> views, Size contentSize)
493	    {
494	        var neededLayout = false;
495	
496	        foreach (View v in views)
497	        {
498	            if (!v.NeedsLayout)
499	            {
500	                continue;
501	            }
502	            neededLayout = true;
503	            v.Layout (contentSize);
504	        }
505	
506	        return neededLayout;
507	    }

528	            return false;

534	        SetNeedsDraw ();
535	
536	        return true;

17	if (condition)
18	using (var obj = Create ())
19	
20	// WRONG ✗ - SCAN FOR THESE
21	void MyMethod()           // Missing space before ()
22	int result = Calculate(x, y);
23	var items = GetItems();
24	if(condition)
25	using(var obj = Create())
26	```
27	
28	**Scan pattern:** Look for `\w(` (word character followed immediately by `(`)
29	
30	### Space Before Brackets ⚠️ #2 MISTAKE
31	```csharp
32	// CORRECT ✓
33	var value = array [index];
34	var item = list [0];
35	MyArray [i] = value;
36	
37	// WRONG ✗ - SCAN FOR THESE
38	var value = array[index];  // Missing space before [
39	var item = list[0];
40	MyArray[i] = value;
41	```
42	
43	**Scan pattern:** Look for `\w[` (word character followed immediately by `[`)
44	
45	### Braces on Next Line ⚠️ #3 MISTAKE
46	```csharp
47	// CORRECT ✓
48	void MyMethod ()
49	{
50	    if (condition)
51	    {
52	        DoWork ();
53	    }
54	}
55	
56	// WRONG ✗ - SCAN FOR THESE
57	void MyMethod() {          // Brace on same line
58	void MyMethod () {         // Brace on same line
59	    if (condition) {       // Brace on same line
60	        DoWork();
61	    }
62	}
63	```
64	
65	**Scan pattern:** Look for `) {` or `= {` (brace on same line)
66	
67	### Blank Lines ⚠️ #4 MISTAKE
68	```csharp
69	// CORRECT ✓ - blank line BEFORE control transfer
70	DoWork ();
71	
72	return result;  // Blank line above
73	
74	// CORRECT ✓ - blank line AFTER control block
75	if (condition)
76	{
77	    DoWork ();
78	}
79	
80	DoNext ();  // Blank line above
81	
82	// WRONG ✗ - SCAN FOR THESE
83	DoWork ();
84	return result;  // No blank line above return
85	
86	if (condition) { DoWork (); }
87	DoNext ();  // No blank line after control block
88	```
89	
90	**Control transfer statements:** `return`, `break`, `continue`, `throw`
91	**Control blocks:** `if`, `for`, `while`, `foreach`, `using`, `try`/`catch`
92	
93	### Indentation
94	```csharp
95	// CORRECT ✓ - 4 spaces per level
96	public class MyClass
97	{
98	    public void MyMethod ()
99	    {
100	        if (condition)
101	        {
102	            DoWork ();
103	        }
104	    }
105	}
106	
107	// WRONG ✗ - tabs or wrong spacing
108	public class MyClass
109	{
110		public void MyMethod ()  // Tab instead of spaces
111		{
112	    if (condition)           // 2 spaces instead of 4
113	    {
114	```
115	
116	**Rule:** 4 spaces per indentation level. NO tabs.
117	
118	## Part 2: Code Style Violations
119	
120	### No `var` for Non-Built-In Types
121	```csharp
122	// CORRECT ✓
123	Label label = new () { Text = "Hello" };
124	List<View> views = [];
125	Window window = new ();
126	var count = 0;       // OK - int is built-in
127	var text = "hello";  // OK - string is built-in
128	
129	// WRONG ✗
130	var label = new Label { Text = "Hello" };
131	var views = new List<View>();
132	var window = new Window();
133	```
134	
135	**Built-in types where var is OK:** `int`, `string`, `bool`, `double`, `float`, `decimal`, `char`, `byte`
136	
137	### Target-Typed New
138	```csharp
139	// CORRECT ✓
140	Label label = new () { Text = "Hello" };
141	Window window = new ();
142	
143	// WRONG ✗
144	Label label = new Label() { Text = "Hello" };
145	Window window = new Window();
146	```
147	
148	### Collection Expressions
149	```csharp
150	// CORRECT ✓
151	List<string> items = ["one", "two", "three"];
152	AllSuggestions = ["word1", "word2", "word3"];
153	return [];
154	
155	// WRONG ✗
156	List<string> items = new () { "one", "two", "three" };
157	AllSuggestions = new () { "word1", "word2", "word3" };
158	return new List<string>();
159	```
160	
161	### Lambda Parameter Discards
162	```csharp
163	// CORRECT ✓
164	textField.TextChanged += (_, _) => { /* ... */ };
165	button.Accepting += (_, args) => ProcessArgs (args);
166	
167	// WRONG ✗ - unused parameters
168	textField.TextChanged += (sender, e) => { /* ... */ };
169	textField.TextChanged += (s, prev) => { /* ... */ };
170	```
171	
172	### Terminology
173	```csharp
174	// CORRECT ✓ - containment relationship
175	View superView = new ();
176	View subView = new ();
177	superView.Add (subView);  // subView.SuperView == superView
178	
179	// WRONG ✗ - for containment
180	View parent = new ();     // Should be: superView
181	View child = new ();      // Should be: subView
182	parent.Add (child);
183	
184	// Comments:
185	// Add the button as a SubView of the window
186	// The dialog's SuperView is the application
187	
188	// WRONG ✗ - in comments
189	// Add the button as a child of the window
190	// The dialog's parent is the application
191	```
192	
193	**Use SuperView/SubView for containment.** Parent/Child only for non-containment references (rare).
194	
195	## Part 3: Quick Scan Commands
196	
197	Use these bash commands to catch common violations:
198	
199	```bash
200	# Missing space before ( - most common error
201	grep -nE '\w\(' YourFile.cs | grep -v '//'
202	
203	# Missing space before [
204	grep -nE '\w\[' YourFile.cs | grep -v '//'
205	
206	# Brace on same line
207	grep -n ') {' YourFile.cs
208	grep -n '= {' YourFile.cs
209	
210	# Var usage (manually verify each is built-in type)
211	grep -n '\bvar\b' YourFile.cs
212	
213	# Trailing whitespace
214	grep -n ' $' YourFile.cs
215	```
216	
217	## Part 4: Manual Review
218	
219	After automated checks, manually review:
220	
221	1. **Every method call/declaration** - space before `()`?
222	2. **Every array access** - space before `[]`?
223	3. **Every opening brace** - on next line?
224	4. **Every return statement** - blank line before it?
225	5. **Every control block** - blank line after it?
226	
227	## Validation Frequency
228	
229	Use this checklist:
230	- ✅ After generating any new class or file
231	- ✅ After modifying existing methods (scan modified lines)
232	- ✅ After completing any task that involves code generation
233	- ✅ Before creating a commit or pull request
234	- ✅ When the code "looks wrong" visually
235	
236	## Why This Matters
237	
238	Formatting violations:
239	- Create noise in code reviews
240	- Violate project CI/CD checks
241	- Make code inconsistent with the rest of Terminal.Gui
242	- Are the #1 complaint about AI-generated code
243	- Are easily preventable with this checklist
244	
245	**The space-before-parentheses style is unusual compared to most C# projects, making it the most commonly violated rule.**
246
